forecast.name: Overnight
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 54. Southwest wind around 9 mph.

forecast.name: Independence Day
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly sunny. High near 65, with temperatures falling to around 62 in the afternoon. West southwest wind 7 to 13 mph, with gusts as high as 18 mph.

weather_report:
As the moon drapes its silver veil over the city, a gentle blanket of clouds settles upon San Francisco, whispering the secrets of the night. The temperature hovers around a comforting 54 degrees, while a light breeze from the southwest stirs the air just enough to remind you that summer is in full swing. The early morning gives way to a day that promises mostly sunny skies, although the afternoon may reveal the familiar peaks and valleys of temperature, retreating to a cooler embrace of 62 degrees—an invitation to stay close and enjoy the languid pleasures of July Fourth.

Expect a mingling of fog along the edges of the horizon, where the marine layer lingers like a shy friend at a gathering, casting a delicate softness to the outlines of the iconic Golden Gate Bridge. The moods of the day dance between sunlit warmth and the cool caress of the Pacific’s breath, an elegant equilibrium that suggests sensible layering for those venturing outdoors. A light jacket or cardigan would serve one well, especially should the wind—modest yet persistent—sweep through the streets.

Activities abound in this pleasant mix of sunshine and cool whispers. Perhaps a leisurely stroll through Golden Gate Park would be in order, or a thoughtful visit to one of the city’s myriad cafes, where warm pastries beckon alongside the rich aromas of coffee. And as the sun dips lower, one might consider finding a cozy spot to watch the evening unfurl. So here lies San Francisco this Independence Day—inviting, vibrant, yet contemplative, a spirit that resonates deeply within us all as we celebrate in our own quiet, yet profound, ways.
